title: Anvil Workbench reviewed plugin catalog v1
description: A reviewed, version-pinned, operator-deployed registry projection of the plugins Workbench may orchestrate. It is not a live marketplace and not a command manifest: every object is closed (additionalProperties:false) and there is no field able to carry a raw shell command, an arbitrary URL/endpoint, a local filesystem path, a generic code/executable body, or a credential value. Each plugin is signed by the operator/owner key as the sole trusted signer and is tamper-evident through `plugin_digest`/`catalog_digest`. Every tool declares a mandatory, machine-checkable effect class and gate set, typed input/output schemas, an opaque host-owned credential reference, and explicitly declared data and host access scopes — an undeclared scope is unrepresentable. A `tool_kind` discriminator gives plugin tools an explicit type that is non-equivalent to an Anvil provider operation (which carries `execution`/`operation_digest`) or a bridge skill (which carries a digest-pinned local body). All prose is untrusted display data, never control instructions.

scopeName.description: A declared, named access scope. The pattern forbids '://', ':', and '/', so an arbitrary URL, endpoint, or filesystem path can never masquerade as a declared scope.

preferenceField.description: R004 (UserValves analog): one NON-SECRET, actor-selectable plugin preference field. It carries a type, optional bounds/allowed values, a safe default, and the actor-selectable scope it resolves at (per_turn/actor/project). It has NO value/secret/token/host field, so a credential or a connector-host endpoint is not representable here; the catalog validator additionally REJECTS any field marked (sensitivity=secret / path_like) or DETECTED (a credential/host-shaped name or a secret/endpoint/path-shaped default) as secret-bearing, so a secret can never be actor-selectable. The 'name' pattern forbids ':' and '/', so a host:port or a URL/path cannot masquerade as a field name.

hostAccess.description: A declared, named host-access scope. Egress is either none or mediated wholly by the plugin host; there is deliberately no URL, endpoint, host, or port field, so an arbitrary-URL fetch is unrepresentable.

credential.description: Credential handling is reference-only. A plugin never carries a credential value; when it needs one, it names an owning connector/credential host and opaque references. There is no value, token, key, or password field anywhere.

gates.description: Mandatory, machine-checkable gate set for every tool (criterion 2).

approval_action.description: A tool gate can only bind the tool-invocation approval action. The lifecycle actions (install_plugin/upgrade_plugin/downgrade_plugin) are request-kind gates, never tool gates; constraining this enum to invoke_effect_tool|null keeps a catalog tool from declaring a dead lifecycle gate that validate_plugin_request would never honour.

tool.description: One reviewed plugin tool. `tool_kind` is the explicit type discriminator (criterion 3): a plugin tool is not an Anvil provider operation and not a bridge skill. The closed object has no execution/bridge_adapter/operation_digest field (which would make it an operation) and no skill body/path field (which would make it a skill).

tool_kind.description: criterion 3: the non-equivalent type. `read_only_connector` marks a tool compiled from a digest-pinned OpenAPI document at review time (R016) and is constrained to the read effect class.

effect.description: Mandatory effect class (criterion 2). There is deliberately no policy_mutation, code_execution, or bounded_execution class: a plugin tool can never change policy or run generic code.

cancellation.description: R012: how an in-flight external call behaves when the plugin is disabled or removed. `unknown_outcome` routes to reconciliation rather than a false success.

runtime.description: R002: the declared runtime and version window. An unsupported runtime version fails a downstream host compatibility check machine-checkably.

openapi_source.description: R016: provenance that this plugin's read-only connector tools were compiled from a digest-pinned OpenAPI document at review time. It carries the reviewed document's digest only — never a live URL or the document body.

provenance.description: R001: the operator/owner key is the sole trusted signer. `signer_kind` is const-pinned so a browser- or hub-supplied catalog signed by any other authority is unrepresentable. Signatures are intentionally deferred (see DIGESTING.md); the operator's configured local catalog bytes and this reviewed provenance are the trust root, and the digest is the tamper-evidence.
